frameworks.name: population decoding
frameworks.one_line: single-trial population activity decoded to read out elapsed and remaining time

frameworks.name: neural population dynamics
frameworks.one_line: decision computations as low-dimensional dynamical motifs in corticostriatal circuits

frameworks.name: ramp-vs-bump temporal coding
frameworks.one_line: time encoding decomposed into monotonic ramps and transient bumps

frameworks.name: state-transition view of commitment
frameworks.one_line: decision commitment as a transition between low-dimensional neural subspaces

core_question: How are evidence evaluation, time tracking, and decision commitment distributed across rat frontal cortex and dorsal striatum populations?
key_assumptions: FOF and ADS jointly support perceptual decision making
population activity carries decodable temporal signals on single trials
manipulations: task epoch (evidence evaluation vs decision report)
temporal reference frame (retrospective from stimulus vs prospective to report)
key_findings: both FOF and ADS robustly decoded retrospective and prospective time on single trials
time encoding decomposed into ramp-like and bump-like dynamical motifs
FOF showed more pronounced decision-aligned changes near report than ADS
stable evidence-evaluation subspaces transitioned at commitment, more so in FOF
interpretations: frontostriatal populations share temporal coding but diverge at decision commitment
FOF subspace transitions may reflect a state change marking commitment
